header.stance: 核心通膨轉弱，但能源價格與存續期間供給穩健，實質殖利率因而維持高檔

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 開篇固定收益論點

6月10日收盤延續了前一日美國公債上漲後仍屹立不搖的高實質利率格局，但其表現形式重新轉向小幅熊市趨陡。10 年期殖利率收於 4.55%，2 年期則維持在 4.13%。基礎通膨轉弱，讓聯準會在採取行動前擁有更多時間，但並未恢復寬鬆路徑。較長天期債券仍須面對堅挺能源價格、擴張性財政背景，以及持續競逐存續期間承接能力的全球供給時程來完成出清。

3個管道解釋了本日收盤。核心消費者通膨低於預期，但在強勁就業報告後，交換市場仍定價 Fed 將於 12月前升息。因此，市場將這份數據視為可以等待的許可，而非政策限制很快便可放鬆的證據。10 年期實質殖利率與損益兩平通膨率均小幅上升，逆轉 6月9日部分通膨溢價緩解，顯示實質折現率疑慮與能源限制均未消失。

美國公債供給提供了主要穩定力量。指標 10 年期增額發行的得標殖利率略低於發行前交易參考殖利率，間接投標人參與度異常強勁，交易商餘額則很小。儘管 5月聯邦赤字超出預期，投資人仍在偏高的殖利率結構下承接存續期間。這次標售解答了附息債供給是否會放大當日總體不確定性的眼前問題，但赤字及創紀錄的全球主權聯合承銷發債，仍使中期期限溢價壓力完好無損。

風險資產使訊號更難解讀，而非更容易。股市大幅下跌、波動率上升，但美國公債殖利率並未下降；原油上漲，美元則幾乎不變。信用基金小幅走弱，但已完成的交易顯示，資本仍可供財務強健或結構完善的借款人使用。因此，前一收盤日的選擇性承接主題，與其核心限制同步延續：通膨可以在邊際上放緩，卻未必能降低為公共赤字、AI 投資及相互競爭的全球發債提供融資所需的實質報酬。

## 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債虧損隨到期年限拉長而增加。2 年期殖利率持平於 4.13%，5 年期上升1個基點至 4.27%，10 年期與 30 年期殖利率各上升2個基點至 4.55% 與 5.03%。此走勢逆轉 6月9日近乎平行的漲勢，但並未重現就業報告後前端的猛烈拋售。政策敏感型殖利率維持在重新定價後的水準附近，中期與長存續期間則承擔邊際調整。

殖利率曲線以受控方式趨陡。2s10s 斜率擴大2個基點至 42，2s30s 也擴大2個基點至 90。2s5s10s 蝶式利差持平於-14，未出現新的腹部錯位；2s10s30s 蝶式利差則上升2個基點至-6，因 10 年期相較兩翼小幅落後。以1個月計，2s10s 仍趨平6個基點，2s30s 趨平 15 個基點，因此單日趨陡仍只是較廣泛、前端主導的緊縮重新定價中的調整。

較長期間的比較進一步強化此延續性。2 年期殖利率1週上升5個基點，1個月上升 23 個基點。5 年期分別上升6個與 25 個基點，相較之下，10 年期為6個與 17 個基點，30 年期為4個與8個基點。前端與腹部的累積漲幅最大，因其對預期政策路徑的曝險最高。長債的單日下跌，則反映殖利率超過 5% 所需的剩餘期限、財政及通膨補償。

通膨拆解顯示反應喜憂參半，而非偏鴿。10 年期 TIPS 實質殖利率上升1個基點至 2.21%，10 年期損益兩平通膨率上升1個基點至 2.34%。5 年期實質殖利率增加1個基點至 1.83%，7 年期上升2個基點至 2.02%，20 年期與 30 年期實質殖利率分別上升2個與3個基點至 2.58% 與 2.78%。實質折現率與通膨補償均推動名目債券遭拋售，其中最遠端實質殖利率曲線的單日升幅最大。

5月 CPI的漲勢廣度帶來緩解，但通膨水準並未令人放心。核心價格較 4月上升 0.2%，低於 0.3%的市場共識，顯示油價衝擊尚未轉化為全面性的月度推力。然而，整體通膨較1年前達到 4.2%，為3年來最快。儘管核心數據轉弱降低了提早行動的壓力，交易員仍在交換市場保留 12月升息的定價。數據公布後，2 年期殖利率曾跌向 4.11%，其後回升至持平收盤。

市場反應調和了核心通膨轉弱與持續緊縮押注之間的表面衝突。強勁就業已消除為勞動市場提供保險的必要，而整體通膨仍遠高於目標。單月有利的核心數據讓決策者可以觀察傳導過程，卻無法確定能源、薪資及需求是否會使目前 3.50%-3.75%的政策利率區間維持充分。2 年期持平收盤捕捉了這種停頓，較遠端實質殖利率上升則維持市場對高中性利率與資本需求的假設。

國庫券顯示現金狀況秩序良好，但接近1年處隱含更多政策限制。4 週期票息等值殖利率上升1個基點至 3.67%，6 週期增加1個基點至 3.68%，8 週期持平於 3.68%。13 週期與 26 週期殖利率維持在 3.72% 與 3.81%，52 週期則維持在 3.90%。極前端整體穩定，並不支持融資市場受到干擾；殖利率向1年期上斜，仍符合已失去早前寬鬆傾向的政策路徑。

$390億的 10 年期增額發行交出乾淨俐落的需求結果。得標殖利率為 4.538%，較 4.539%的發行前交易殖利率低 0.1 個基點，投標倍數為 2.57。間接投標人取得獲接受競爭性投標的 78.21%，直接投標人占 12.32%，交易商占 9.46%。票息 4-3/8%的債券於 6月15日交割。強勁的終端投資人參與，避免盤中長端小幅走弱演變成標售引發的破位，但交割仍是獨立的現金事件，不能視為已包含在投標統計中的證據。

財政數據使標售後的供給問題仍然存在。聯邦政府 5月錄得 $2926.5億赤字，高於 $2,695億的市場共識，此前 4月為 $2150.2億盈餘。收入為 $3355.1億，支出為 $6281.6億，使本財政年度迄今的赤字達到 $1.246兆。數據並未指出當日存在融資問題，但確實強化了1項理由：當累積借款需求仍然龐大時，個別標售成功未必會壓低期限溢價。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

信用資產總報酬隨政府公債殖利率上升及股市走弱而下滑。LQD 下跌 0.2306%，HYG 下跌 0.1884%，JNK 下跌 0.1981%。現券投資等級市場僅呈現溫和壓力，而非廣泛受損：一宗創紀錄交易後，加拿大公司債利差擴大約2個基點，但相較政府公債殖利率曲線仍僅高出 84.5 個基點，較其5年平均緊縮逾 30%。融資管道仍然暢通，但新增借款的規模已開始排擠其他風險資產。

Amazon的 C$140億 5部分發債，是資產負債表排擠效應最清楚的例子。該交易於週一定價，是歷來規模最大的加幣公司債，並使 Amazon 成為當地指數前 10 大發行人。僅其 C$47.5億的 30 年期券，就超過一般 C$30億的加拿大政府長債標售。基金經理人出售公用事業債、Alphabet 債券及其他高品質證券來騰出空間，使超大規模雲端業者的資本需求直接連結至主權債與公司債的相對價值壓力。

市場承接了這筆發債，但並非未改變近期發債環境。交易商預期，其他大型借款人將在投資組合重建承接能力時延後發債；交易前後，加拿大長天期政府公債相對可比美國公債已表現落後。考量交易規模及強勁基金流入，約2個基點的利差擴大相當溫和，但仍顯示市場准入與外部效應可以並存：高評級借款人能大規模執行交易，同時提高相鄰發行人的出清利差，並消耗長存續期間需求。

Amazon 另透過由 Citigroup 主導的 $175億無擔保延遲動用定期貸款，新增銀行融資管道。該額度可使用至 9月底，每次借款均有3年償還期。依公司高順位無擔保評級，定價為 SOFR 加 62.5 至 87.5 個基點。此貸款可作為潛在 AI 股權投資承諾的過渡融資，無須立即發債，但仍維持資料中心、模型開發商及相關基礎設施融資需求集中的大趨勢。

評級較低的 AI 基礎設施也獲得大量承接，差異化關鍵在於擔保結構的經濟性。Applied Digital 旗下子公司以 7% 殖利率發行 $15.9億、2031 到期的債券，用於興建另一座出租給 CoreWeave的 North Dakota 設施，低於較早期階段要求的 10%。訂單約達發債規模5倍。15 年租約及市場對 CoreWeave 信用品質看法改善支撐了交易結果，所得款項也用於償還過渡貸款。強勁承接反映合約現金流與租戶風險重新定價，而非對所有 AI 借款人毫無差別的熱情。

CoreWeave的5年期違約保護成本已從 12月的 8.81 個百分點降至 4.52 個百分點，開發商為出租給該公司的專案募集了超過 $80億。CoreWeave 同時準備發行等值 $35億的美元與歐元債券。此組合顯示，市場認定的租戶品質改善，可降低專案層級融資成本並釋出更多供給。這也使投資人暴露於一系列高度相關的假設，包括使用率、客戶集中度，以及快速擴張的基礎設施鏈能否反覆取得資本。

日本信用市場以明顯更高的價格承接併購風險。Nippon Steel 完成收購 US Steel 後第1次發行一般公司債，募集 ¥900億。10 年期券票息為 3.202%，相較 JGB 利差為 54 個基點，是 1998 年以來最大；儘管如此，廣泛的投資人興趣仍讓公司得以擴大交易規模。結果同時體現市場准入與讓利：海外擴張仍可融資，但當地利率上升，加上業務與資產負債表風險增加，要求實質上更大的補償。

主要反向力量存在於最強發行人以下的市場。Pimco 指出，即便高評級利差接近近30年來最緊水準，槓桿融資與私募信貸中的到期日展延及實物支付結構仍在增加。這項警告符合當日行情的分化。Amazon 可跨幣別及銀行貸款分散融資，受保障的資料中心專案也可大幅收緊重新定價；較弱借款人若依賴以債養債進行再融資，則面臨更大的損失嚴重程度。信用市場仍然暢通，但其表面平靜奠基於極不均等的融資彈性。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

日本對超長存續期間所要求的價格，發出已開發市場最清晰的警訊。30 年期 JGB 標售投標倍數為 2.94，低於前次的 3.49，也低於過去1年 3.4的平均值。標售後債券期貨擴大跌幅。殖利率回落後，投資人的買進意願下降，儘管通膨、財政政策不確定性及央行可能減少購債，仍支持更高的長端溢價。

這次標售透過國際買方基礎影響美國公債。就在美國長端殖利率仍高於 5% 之際，日本投資人面對日益上升的國內替代選擇，使相對配置決策對匯率避險與波動率更加敏感。市場仍強烈預期 BOJ 將進一步緊縮。總裁 Kazuo Ueda 住院使主持會議及溝通職責轉由副手承擔，但並未實質改變決策委員會層級的政策預期，因此制度延續性限制了其暫時缺席對全球存續期間資產帶來的任何支撐。

主權供給廣泛存在，並非美國獨有。各國政府在 2026 年透過聯合承銷市場發行創紀錄的 $5,040億，超過疫情期間的同期速度。義大利占近 €700億，德國放寬國防與基礎設施的財政規則後已募集 €140億。若計入定期標售，實際公共借款總額遠高於此。強勁需求，尤其是較短天期需求，讓債務管理機構得以提前融資，但隨戰爭、通膨及央行不確定性升高，投資人正要求更高殖利率。

再融資解釋了大部分發債時程，但並未消除其市場影響。歐元區主權國家透過聯合承銷進行的再融資較1年前增加 26%，同期聯合承銷總發行量則增加 11%。即使到期贖回減少，5月發行量仍加速，顯示部分發行人在潛在升息前機會性提前融資。對美國公債而言，傳導途徑在於競爭：每宗獲得良好承接的歐洲交易都確認全球儲蓄仍然可用，但日益增加的高評級替代資產，也提高了留住跨境買方所需的殖利率。

中國 5月通膨數據帶來另一種政策張力。消費者價格較上月下降 0.1%，較1年前上升 1.2%，低於 1.4%的市場共識；核心通膨則放緩至 1.1%。在能源、金屬及工業投入品維持堅挺之際，生產者價格月增 0.5%、年增 3.9%，高於 3.5%的市場共識。消費動能疲弱支持政策扶持，但上游成本轉強，限制政策扶持轉化為區域殖利率下降或貨幣走弱的容易程度。

整體而言，全球殖利率曲線在成長不均下同時面對供給與通膨。中國消費需求疲弱，加上較短天期主權聯合承銷仍有強勁需求，避免了全面撤出存續期間資產。JGB 標售及創紀錄發行速度則顯示，買方承接能力有其價格，尤其在長天期。6月10日美國殖利率曲線趨陡，因此符合全球出清競爭的格局：沒有單一海外事件主導美國公債收盤，但海外替代選擇與財政計畫，使核心 CPI 疲軟後的長端門檻無法下降。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場債券面臨共同背景小幅收緊。EMB 下跌 0.3345%，美元指數小升 0.04%，美國公債實質殖利率上升，2項原油指標均上漲。美元變動很小，因此壓力並非全面性的貨幣緊縮。更高基準殖利率與油價反而加深了不同經濟體之間的差異：具可信財政及外匯存底緩衝者，以及利用昂貴國內政策防衛匯率者。

印尼在劇烈拋售後實現部分逆轉。其 10 年期政府公債殖利率下跌 15 個基點至 7.26%，此前2日曾上升多達 64 個基點。印尼盾曾升值 0.9%，股市上漲 3.4%。央行與財政部的安撫言論減緩單向拋售，但年內外國投資人仍從債券淨撤出 $5.94億，從股票撤出 $37億。市場已開始穩定，但信心尚未恢復至先前水準。

政策設計解釋了仍然存在的溢價。印尼央行表示將容許債券殖利率上升以支撐印尼盾、不買進 10 年期以上證券、透過附買回工具抽走銀行流動性，並降低外國投資人的避險成本。財政部長重申赤字占 GDP 3%的法定上限。這些措施強化匯率防衛架構，卻也提高政府融資成本。身為石油淨進口國，印尼同時還須承擔更高的燃料補貼支出。

土耳其現行立場呈現另一種美元流動性壓力。央行已暫停透過 37% 附買回利率工具放款，並將實際融資轉向 40%的隔夜利率。自戰爭開始以來，當局為抵抗里拉走弱，外匯存底總額已減少 $200億；年通膨也已連續2個月加速。實質上緊縮的融資環境可支撐貨幣，但外匯存底流失與昂貴能源，使平衡經濟活動放緩與物價穩定的空間縮小。

南非早已正式調高政策利率。央行於 5月升息 25 個基點至 7%，為3年來首次，並將第3季通膨預測由 3.7% 上調至 4.9%。官員警告，中東衝突若延長，可能增加家庭壓力、違約及不良貸款。其主權傳導路徑是由輸入型能源價格傳至通膨預期及利率，再傳入銀行資產品質與信用擴張，而非美元立即短缺。

阿根廷僅在紐約截止時間後出現有利的債務管理發展。S&P 以財政盈餘、外匯存底累積及外部流動性改善為由，將主權評級由 CCC+ 上調至 B-，展望穩定。央行年內已購入超過 $100億外匯存底，而本地美元發債及多邊融資已涵蓋近期需求。由於此行動發生於完整收盤後，不能解釋 EMB 下跌；但確實改善了未來重返國際發債與負債管理的路徑。

## 跨資產

股票發出明確的避險訊號，卻未能帶動美國公債上漲。S&P 500 下跌 1.6199%，Nasdaq 100 下跌 1.9821%，半導體指數下跌 3.5658%。VIX 上升 11.8269% 至 22.22。市場衡量高實質利率及 AI 擴張的融資密集程度之際，科技股再度表現落後。股市跌勢的廣度本應支撐存續期間資產，因此長天期美國公債殖利率同步上升，更凸顯供給與通膨反向力量的強度。

能源強化了損益兩平通膨率反轉。WTI 上漲 2.0748% 至 $90.03，Brent 上漲 1.8043% 至 $93.10。美國商業原油庫存減少 722.7萬桶，遠超過市場共識預期的 397.4萬桶降幅，使庫存降至 4.265億桶，較5年季節性平均低約 5%。包括戰略石油儲備在內的石油總庫存減少 1,350萬桶，煉油設備利用率達到 95.3%。儘管核心 CPI 轉弱，實體供需平衡仍使近期能源通膨風險持續存在。

美元與黃金否定了傳統避險格局。美元指數僅上升 0.04% 至 99.95，幾乎沒有全球再度爭搶美元的跡象；黃金期貨則下跌 3.5634% 至 $4,108.20。當主導性衝擊是實質折現率上升，而非系統性恐慌時，股市下跌、波動率上升與黃金走弱可以並存。美元近乎持平，也有助於解釋為何新興市場壓力集中在個別國家的基本面，而未演變成一致性的硬通貨資產清算。

信用資產的小幅虧損補全了此一混合格局。投資等級與高收益基金同向下跌，但跌幅遠小於股票，初級市場交易也持續以差異化條件吸引買方。因此，跨資產表現挑戰任何對本日收盤的簡單防禦性解讀。風險偏好轉弱，但油價與實質殖利率阻礙政府公債受惠；資本市場仍然正常運作，但其為大型公共計畫與科技計畫提供融資的能力本身，也維持了對存續期間的競爭。

## 前瞻市場意義

6月11日 08:30 ET 公布的美國 PPI 與初領失業救濟金人數，將檢驗核心 CPI 疲軟能否維持為暫停訊號，而非新一波存續期間資產支撐的起點。整體 PPI 月增率預期由 1.4% 放緩至 0.7%，核心則由 1.0% 降至 0.5%；初領人數在前值 225,000 後，預期落於 216,000-220,000 區間。可觀察的觸發因素，是生產者價格漲勢廣度是否在初領人數未再下降的情況下收斂。上游通膨轉弱且勞動需求疲軟，將確認 Fed 有等待空間，並挑戰 2 年期至 10 年期實質殖利率上升。核心 PPI 堅挺且初領人數偏低，則將確認 6月10日的 CPI 緩解範圍狹窄，維持 12月升息的解讀，並重啟殖利率曲線腹部壓力。

6月11日的融資時序將檢驗 10 年期增額發行的強勁終端投資人需求，能否延伸至現金與長存續期間兩端。美國財政部將於 11:30 ET 各標售 $750億的 4 週期與 8 週期國庫券，於 13:00 增額發行 $220億的 30 年期債券，並可能於 13:40 至 14:00 買回最多 $125億的近期名目附息債。可觀察的觸發因素包括長債得標殖利率相對發行前交易殖利率的表現、投標人占比及交易商承接比例，其後再分別觀察國庫券需求與 6月15日附息債交割。長端投標強勁且國庫券秩序良好，將確認由總體因素主導的趨陡，並部分化解對買方承接能力的疑慮。若通膨溫和但長債結果疲弱，將挑戰此一解釋，並使財政供給重新成為獨立的期限溢價驅動因素。

ECB 於 6月11日的決策、土耳其同日的政策決策，以及 6月16日結束的 BOJ 會議，構成全球政策考驗。基準情境情境為 ECB 升息 25 個基點，使存款利率升至 2.25%；土耳其附買回利率維持 37%，儘管實際融資成本接近 40%；BOJ 則升息 25 個基點至接近 1.00%。可觀察的觸發因素包括後續行動指引、德國公債與 JGB 殖利率曲線反應、里拉與日圓走勢，以及 BOJ 購債計畫。指引受控且貨幣穩定，將挑戰海外緊縮必然持續推高美國公債出清利率的觀點。鷹派政策接續、長端失序拋售或外匯存底壓力再現，則將確認全球對存續期間的競爭，並使石油進口主權國家的輸入型通膨風險再度升高。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為基準。

overall_summary.headline: 核心 CPI 疲軟未能撼動高實質殖利率，美國公債殖利率曲線熊市趨陡
overall_summary.session_narrative: 截至完整紐約收盤，美國公債殖利率曲線小幅熊市趨陡，前端持穩，較長天期殖利率上升。核心 CPI 疲軟讓 Fed 有等待空間，但持續存在的升息定價、較堅挺的油價，以及上升的實質殖利率與損益兩平通膨率，阻礙了存續期間資產上漲。強勁的 10 年期標售需求穩定了盤勢；龐大財政需求、全球發債及融資密集型投資則使長端出清利率維持高檔。
overall_summary.summary: 前端維持在就業報告後重新定價的水準附近，較長天期美國公債則走弱，高實質利率格局延續。核心 CPI 轉弱為 Fed 爭取了時間，但油價、財政供給及全球對存續期間的競爭，阻礙更廣泛的緩解。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤格局顯示，政策限制集中於前端，供給風險則集中於存續期間。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注 6月11日的通膨、勞動市場及標售結果，是否驗證前端穩定。
section_summaries.summary: 核心通膨疲軟讓 Fed 有等待空間，但並未重啟寬鬆路徑，使前端持穩，而較遠端的實質殖利率與損益兩平通膨率上升。強勁的 10 年期標售需求抑制了賣壓，但能源、赤字及全球發債仍維持期限溢價壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 2 年期殖利率持穩、長存續期間資產走弱，強化 Fed 觀望路徑。
section_summaries.next_check: 30 年期標售將顯示終端投資人需求是否能從 10 年期區段向外延伸。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 政策敏感型殖利率維持在就業報告後重新定價的水準附近，虧損隨到期年限拉長而增加，殖利率曲線小幅趨陡。實質殖利率與損益兩平通膨率共同推升名目殖利率；同時，秩序良好的國庫券市場、強勁的 10 年期增額發行，以及擴大的財政赤字，凸顯短期承接能力與持續供給壓力的差異。

section_summaries.market_read: 發債功能正常限制了系統性疑慮，但集中的融資需求提高出清成本。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注投資組合重建存續期間承接能力之際，新交易是否需要更大的讓利。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 隨政府公債殖利率上升，信用資產小幅走弱，但資產負債表強健或現金流受保障的借款人，仍能在初級市場完成大型交易。Amazon的融資與 AI 基礎設施發債顯示，市場准入仍然暢通，但排擠效應、新券讓利及較弱借款人的再融資風險正變得更不均衡。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球買方承接能力仍在，但海外替代選擇使美國長端門檻維持高檔。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注德國公債與 JGB 殖利率曲線反應，尋找全球儲蓄競爭加劇的證據。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 日本超長天期標售需求疲弱，加上主權聯合承銷發債創紀錄，凸顯全球存續期間資產只有在更高出清價格下才有需求。海外政策緊縮、財政擴張及相互競爭的高評級供給，使美國核心通膨轉弱、全球成長不均之際，美國公債期限溢價仍承受壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場壓力仍因國而異，其中能源進口國最容易受到融資環境收緊衝擊。
section_summaries.next_check: 監測里拉、印尼盾及外匯存底趨勢，觀察匯率防衛是否失去成效。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 新興市場債券面臨更高基準殖利率與油價，但並未出現廣泛的美元緊縮，因此國內緩衝與政策可信度成為關鍵。印尼透過匯率防衛措施穩定局勢；土耳其與南非則顯示，外匯存底運用、高昂融資成本及輸入型通膨，如何將壓力轉移至主權融資與信用。

section_summaries.market_read: 當通膨與實質利率占主導地位時，避險情緒不再保證能支撐存續期間資產。
section_summaries.next_check: 關注股市再度走弱時，實質殖利率會下降，還是長端持續遭到拋售。
section_summaries.summary: 股票與科技股在波動率上升之際遭到拋售，但由於油價與實質殖利率走高，美國公債未能上漲。美元近乎持平、黃金下跌，指向實質折現率衝擊，而非系統性恐慌；信用資產僅小幅下跌則顯示資本市場仍正常運作。

section_summaries.market_read: 接下來的訊號將決定這波趨陡是總體因素所致，還是獨立的供給衝擊。
section_summaries.next_check: 比較 6月11日的數據、標售結果及全球政策反應，直至 6月16日 BOJ 收盤。
section_summaries.summary: 6月11日至16日的3項考驗將區分暫時停頓與存續期間資產再度獲得支撐：美國通膨與勞動數據、美國公債融資需求，以及全球央行指引。其市場觸發因素涵蓋實質殖利率、標售品質、海外殖利率曲線與貨幣，可直接檢驗總體限制或供給競爭何者占主導地位。

watch_events.market_implication: 前者挑戰較高實質殖利率；後者維持升息定價與殖利率曲線腹部壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 生產者價格漲勢廣度收斂且初領人數轉弱，或核心 PPI 維持堅挺且初領人數保持低檔。
watch_events.title: 美國 PPI 與初領失業救濟金人數
watch_events.watch_line: 檢驗上游通膨轉弱是否伴隨勞動市場再度疲軟。

watch_events.market_implication: 強勁需求抑制期限溢價；疲弱需求則使財政供給重新成為獨立驅動因素。
watch_events.market_trigger: 長債得標殖利率優於市場且國庫券秩序良好，或出現標售尾差並伴隨偏高的交易商承接比例。
watch_events.title: 美國公債券、債券與買回時序
watch_events.watch_line: 評估長債投標、國庫券需求、買回執行情況及 6月15日現金交割。

watch_events.market_implication: 穩定可降低全球出清利率壓力；壓力則確認對存續期間的競爭。
watch_events.market_trigger: 指引受控且貨幣穩定，或鷹派行動伴隨長端失序拋售。
watch_events.title: ECB、土耳其與 BOJ 政策時序
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤政策指引、德國公債與 JGB 殖利率曲線、BOJ 購債計畫，以及里拉與日圓走勢。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The June 10 close preserved the high-real-rate regime that had survived the previous day’s Treasury rally, but shifted its expression back toward a mild bear steepening. The 10-year yield ended at 4.55%, whereas the 2-year held at 4.13%. Softer underlying inflation gave the Federal Reserve more time before acting; it did not restore an easing path. Longer maturities still had to clear against firm energy prices, an expansive fiscal backdrop and a global supply calendar that keeps competing for duration capacity.

3 channels explain the close. Core consumer inflation undershot expectations, yet swaps continued to price a Fed increase by December after the strong employment report. The market therefore treated the release as permission to wait, not as evidence that policy restraint could soon be relaxed. Real yields and breakevens both edged higher at 10 years, reversing part of June 9’s inflation-premium relief and showing that neither the real discount-rate concern nor the energy constraint had disappeared.

Treasury supply supplied the main stabilizer. The benchmark 10-year reopening cleared slightly through its when-issued reference, with unusually strong indirect participation and a small dealer residual. Investors absorbed duration at the elevated yield structure even as the May federal deficit exceeded expectations. The auction resolved the immediate question of whether coupon supply would amplify the day’s macro uncertainty, but the deficit and record global sovereign syndication kept medium-term term-premium pressure intact.

Risk assets made the signal more demanding rather than easier. Equities fell sharply and volatility rose, yet Treasury yields did not rally; crude advanced and the dollar was almost unchanged. Credit funds weakened modestly, although completed transactions showed that capital remained available to strong or well-structured borrowers. The prior close’s selective absorption theme therefore continued alongside its central constraint: inflation can soften at the margin without lowering the real return needed to finance public deficits, AI investment and competing global issuance.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury losses increased with maturity. The 2-year yield was unchanged at 4.13%, the 5-year rose 1 basis point to 4.27%, and the 10- and 30-year yields each increased 2 basis points to 4.55% and 5.03%. The move reversed June 9’s nearly parallel rally without recreating the forceful front-end selloff seen after payrolls. Policy-sensitive yields stayed anchored near their repriced levels, while intermediate and long duration carried the marginal adjustment.

The curve steepened in a controlled fashion. The 2s10s slope widened 2 basis points to 42, and 2s30s widened 2 to 90. The 2s5s10s butterfly was unchanged at -14, leaving no new belly dislocation, whereas the 2s10s30s butterfly rose 2 basis points to -6 as the 10-year underperformed its wings modestly. Over 1 month, 2s10s was still 6 basis points flatter and 2s30s 15 flatter, so the daily steepening remained an adjustment inside a broader front-loaded tightening repricing.

Longer comparisons reinforce that continuity. 2-year yields were 5 basis points higher over 1 week and 23 over 1 month. The 5-year increases were 6 and 25 basis points, compared with 6 and 17 at 10 years and 4 and eight at 30 years. The front and belly retained the largest accumulated move because they carry the greatest exposure to the expected policy path. The long bond’s daily loss instead reflected the residual term, fiscal and inflation compensation needed above 5%.

Inflation decomposition showed a mixed rather than dovish response. The 10-year TIPS real yield rose 1 basis point to 2.21%, and the 10-year breakeven increased 1 to 2.34%. 5-year real yields added 1 basis point to 1.83%, 7-year yields rose 2 to 2.02%, and the 20- and 30-year real yields gained 2 and 3 basis points to 2.58% and 2.78%. Both real discount rates and inflation compensation contributed to the nominal selloff, with the far-end real curve carrying the largest daily increase.

May CPI offered relief on breadth but not on the level of inflation. Core prices rose 0.2% from April, below the 0.3% consensus, indicating that the oil shock had not yet become a generalized monthly impulse. Headline inflation nevertheless reached 4.2% from a year earlier, its fastest pace in 3 years. Traders retained a hike by December in swaps, even though the softer core result reduced pressure for an earlier move. The 2-year yield briefly fell toward 4.11% after the release before returning to its unchanged close.

The reaction reconciles the apparent conflict between softer core inflation and persistent tightening bets. Strong employment had already removed the need for labor-market insurance, and headline inflation remained far above target. A single favorable core month allowed policymakers to observe the pass-through process; it did not settle whether energy, wages and demand will permit the current 3.50%-3.75% policy range to remain sufficient. The flat 2-year close captured that pause, whereas higher real yields farther out preserved the market’s elevated neutral-rate and capital-demand assumptions.

Bills showed orderly cash conditions with more restraint embedded toward 1 year. 4-week coupon-equivalent yields rose 1 basis point to 3.67%, 6-week yields added 1 to 3.68%, and 8-week yields were unchanged at 3.68%. The 13- and 26-week yields held at 3.72% and 3.81%, while the 52-week yield stayed at 3.90%. Stability across the very front end argued against a funding disturbance; the upward slope toward 1 year remained consistent with a policy path that had lost its earlier easing bias.

The $39 billion 10-year reopening delivered a clean demand result. It stopped at 4.538%, 0.1 basis point through the 4.539% when-issued yield, with a 2.57 bid-to-cover ratio. Indirect bidders took 78.21% of accepted competitive awards, direct bidders 12.32% and dealers 9.46%. The 4-3/8% notes settle on June 15. Strong end-user participation prevented the session’s mild long-end cheapening from becoming an auction-led break, but settlement remains a distinct cash event rather than evidence already contained in the bidding statistics.

Fiscal data kept the supply question open beyond the auction. The federal government recorded a $292.65 billion May deficit, wider than the $269.5 billion consensus, after April’s $215.02 billion surplus. Receipts were $335.51 billion and outlays $628.16 billion, taking the fiscal-year-to-date shortfall to $1.246 trillion. The release did not identify a same-day financing problem. It did reinforce why successful individual auctions need not compress term premium when the cumulative borrowing requirement remains large.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Credit total returns softened with government yields and weaker equities. LQD fell 0.2306%, HYG declined 0.1884% and JNK lost 0.1981%. Cash investment-grade conditions showed modest pressure rather than broad impairment: Canadian corporate spreads widened about 2 basis points after a record transaction, yet remained at 84.5 basis points over the government curve, more than 30% tighter than their 5-year average. Funding stayed available, but the scale of new borrowing was beginning to displace other risk.

Amazon’s C$14 billion 5-part sale supplied the clearest example of balance-sheet crowding. The transaction, priced on Monday, was the largest Canadian-dollar corporate bond ever and made Amazon a top-10 issuer in the local index. Its C$4.75 billion 30-year tranche alone exceeded a typical C$3 billion Canadian government long-bond auction. Fund managers sold utilities, Alphabet bonds and other high-quality securities to make room, linking hyperscaler capital demand directly to sovereign and corporate relative-value pressure.

The market absorbed the offering, but not without changing the near-term issuance environment. Dealers expected other large borrowers to delay sales as portfolios rebuilt capacity, and long Canadian government bonds had already underperformed comparable Treasuries around the deal. The roughly 2-basis-point spread widening was mild given the transaction’s size and strong fund inflows. It nonetheless demonstrated that access and externality can coexist: a high-grade borrower can execute at scale while raising the clearing spread for adjacent issuers and consuming long-duration demand.

Amazon added a separate bank-funding channel through a $17.5 billion unsecured delayed-draw term loan led by Citigroup. The facility remains available through the end of September, and each borrowing carries a 3-year repayment period. Pricing ranges from 62.5 to 87.5 basis points over SOFR according to the company’s senior unsecured rating. The loan can bridge prospective AI equity commitments without forcing an immediate bond sale, but it preserves the broader concentration of funding demand in data centers, model developers and related infrastructure.

Lower-rated AI infrastructure also found substantial capacity, with security economics doing the differentiating. An Applied Digital subsidiary raised $1.59 billion of 2031 bonds at a 7% yield to build another North Dakota facility leased to CoreWeave, down from the 10% demanded on an earlier phase. Orders reached about 5 times the deal size. A 15-year lease and improved CoreWeave credit perception supported the result, while the proceeds also repay a bridge loan. Strong absorption reflected contractual cash flow and repricing of tenant risk, not undifferentiated enthusiasm for every AI borrower.

CoreWeave’s 5-year default-protection cost had fallen to 4.52 percentage points from 8.81 in December, and developers had raised more than $8 billion for projects leased to it. CoreWeave was simultaneously preparing a $3.5 billion-equivalent dollar-and-euro bond sale. The combination shows how improving perceived tenant quality can lower project-level funding costs and release more supply. It also leaves investors exposed to correlated assumptions about utilization, customer concentration and repeated access to capital across a rapidly expanding infrastructure chain.

Japanese credit cleared at a visibly higher price for acquisition risk. Nippon Steel raised ¥90 billion in its 1st straight-bond sale since buying US Steel. The 10-year tranche carried a 3.202% coupon and a 54-basis-point spread over JGBs, its widest spread since 1998, even though broad investor interest allowed the company to increase the deal size. The result combined access with concession: overseas expansion remained financeable, but rising local rates and greater business and balance-sheet risk required materially wider compensation.

The main counterforce lies below the strongest issuers. Pimco argued that maturity extensions and payment-in-kind structures were increasing in leveraged and private credit even as high-grade spreads remained near their tightest in almost three decades. That warning fits the completed tape’s dispersion. Amazon could diversify across currencies and bank loans, and a protected data-center project could reprice sharply tighter, whereas weaker borrowers face greater loss severity when refinancing depends on adding debt to debt. Credit remained open, but its apparent calm rested on highly unequal financing flexibility.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Japan supplied the clearest developed-market warning about the price required for super-long duration. A 30-year JGB auction drew a 2.94 bid-to-cover ratio, down from 3.49 previously and below the 3.4 average over the prior year. Bond futures extended declines after the sale. Investors had become less willing to buy after a pullback in yields, even though inflation, fiscal-policy uncertainty and the prospective reduction of central-bank purchases continued to argue for a larger long-end premium.

The auction mattered for Treasuries through the international buyer base. Japanese investors face a rising domestic alternative just as US long yields remain above 5%, making the relative allocation decision more sensitive to currency hedging and volatility. Expectations for another BOJ tightening step remained strong. Governor Kazuo Ueda’s hospitalization shifted chairing and communication duties to his deputies without materially changing the board-level policy expectation, so institutional continuity limited any relief to global duration from his temporary absence.

Sovereign supply was broad rather than uniquely American. Governments had sold a record $504 billion through syndicated markets in 2026, exceeding the comparable pandemic-era pace. Italy accounted for nearly €70 billion, and Germany had raised €14 billion after loosening its fiscal rules for defense and infrastructure. Regular auctions make the true public borrowing total much larger. Strong demand, especially at shorter maturities, allowed debt managers to advance funding, but investors were requiring higher yields as war, inflation and central-bank uncertainty increased.

Refinancing explains much of the calendar without removing its market impact. Euro-area sovereign refinancing through syndications had increased 26% from a year earlier, compared with an 11% rise in total syndicated issuance. May volumes also accelerated even as redemptions declined, indicating some opportunistic front-loading before potential rate increases. For Treasuries, the transmission is competitive: every well-supported European transaction confirms that global savings remain available, yet the growing stock of high-grade alternatives raises the yield needed to retain cross-border buyers.

China’s May inflation data added a different policy tension. Consumer prices fell 0.1% on the month and rose 1.2% from a year earlier, below the 1.4% consensus, while core inflation eased to 1.1%. Producer prices increased 0.5% monthly and 3.9% annually, above the 3.5% consensus, as energy, metals and industrial inputs stayed firm. Weak consumer momentum argues for policy support, but stronger pipeline costs limit the ease with which that support can translate into lower regional yields or a weaker currency.

Taken together, global curves were confronting supply and inflation with uneven growth. Weak consumer demand in China and still-strong demand for shorter sovereign syndications prevented a generalized duration withdrawal. The JGB auction and record issuance pace showed that buyer capacity has a price, particularly at long maturities. June 10’s US steepening therefore fit a contested global clearing process: no single foreign event drove the Treasury close, but overseas alternatives and fiscal programs kept the long-end hurdle from falling after soft core CPI.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt faced a mildly tighter common backdrop. EMB fell 0.3345%, the dollar index edged 0.04% higher, Treasury real yields rose and both crude benchmarks advanced. The dollar move was small, so the pressure was not a generalized currency squeeze. Higher base yields and oil instead reinforced the distinction between economies with credible fiscal and reserve buffers and those using expensive domestic policy to defend exchange rates.

Indonesia achieved a partial reversal after an acute selloff. Its 10-year government yield fell 15 basis points to 7.26% after rising as much as 64 basis points over the prior 2 days. The rupiah gained as much as 0.9%, and equities rose 3.4%. Reassurance from the central bank and finance ministry slowed one-way selling, but foreign investors had still withdrawn a net $594 million from bonds and $3.7 billion from equities during the year. Stabilization had begun without restoring confidence to earlier levels.

Policy design explains the remaining premium. Bank Indonesia signaled that it would tolerate higher bond yields to support the rupiah, refrain from buying securities of 10 years or longer, drain bank liquidity through repurchase facilities and lower foreign investors’ hedging costs. The finance minister reaffirmed the statutory deficit ceiling of 3% of GDP. Those steps sharpen the currency-defense framework, yet they also raise government financing costs. As a net oil importer, Indonesia must absorb a larger fuel-subsidy bill at the same time.

Türkiye’s existing stance revealed a different form of dollar-liquidity pressure. The central bank had paused lending at its 37% repo facility and shifted effective funding toward the 40% overnight rate. Gross foreign-exchange reserves had fallen $20 billion since the war began as authorities resisted lira weakness, and annual inflation had accelerated for 2 months. Tight de facto funding supports the currency, but reserve loss and expensive energy narrow the room to balance slowing activity against price stability.

South Africa had already moved the formal policy rate higher. The reserve bank raised it 25 basis points to 7% in May, its 1st increase in 3 years, and lifted its Q3 inflation forecast to 4.9% from 3.7%. Officials warned that a prolonged Middle East conflict could increase household stress, defaults and nonperforming loans. The sovereign transmission runs from imported energy to inflation expectations and rates, then into bank asset quality and credit extension, rather than through an immediate shortage of dollars.

Argentina supplied a favorable debt-management development only after the New York cutoff. S&P raised the sovereign to B- from CCC+ with a stable outlook, citing fiscal surpluses, reserve accumulation and improved external liquidity. The central bank had purchased more than $10 billion of reserves during the year, while local dollar placements and multilateral financing covered near-term needs. Because the action came after the completed close, it did not explain EMB’s decline; it did improve the prospective path toward international issuance and liability management.

## Cross asset

Equities delivered a clear risk-off signal without producing a Treasury rally. The S&P 500 fell 1.6199%, the Nasdaq 100 lost 1.9821% and the semiconductor index dropped 3.5658%. The VIX rose 11.8269% to 22.22. Technology again underperformed as markets weighed high real rates and the financing intensity of AI expansion. The breadth of the equity decline should have supported duration, so the simultaneous rise in long Treasury yields underscored the strength of the supply and inflation counterforces.

Energy reinforced the breakeven reversal. WTI rose 2.0748% to $90.03 and Brent advanced 1.8043% to $93.10. US commercial crude inventories fell 7.227 million barrels, well beyond the 3.974 million consensus decline, leaving stocks at 426.5 million and about 5% below their seasonal 5-year average. Total petroleum stocks including the Strategic Petroleum Reserve dropped 13.5 million barrels, and refinery utilization reached 95.3%. The physical balance kept near-term energy inflation risk active despite softer core CPI.

The dollar and gold rejected a conventional haven configuration. The dollar index gained just 0.04% to 99.95, offering little sign of a renewed global dollar scramble, while gold futures fell 3.5634% to $4,108.20. Falling equities, rising volatility and lower gold can coexist when the dominant shock is a higher real discount rate rather than systemic fear. The nearly flat dollar also helps explain why EM pressure remained concentrated in country fundamentals instead of becoming a uniform hard-currency liquidation.

Credit’s modest losses completed the mixed pattern. Investment-grade and high-yield funds moved in the same negative direction, but much less than equities, and primary deals continued to attract buyers at differentiated terms. Cross assets therefore challenged any simple defensive reading of the close. Risk appetite weakened, yet oil and real yields prevented government bonds from benefiting; capital markets remained functional, yet their very ability to finance large public and technology programs sustained the competition for duration.

## Forward Market Significance

US PPI and initial jobless claims on June 11 at 08:30 ET will test whether soft core CPI can remain a pause signal rather than the start of renewed duration relief. Headline PPI is expected to slow to 0.7% month over month from 1.4%, and core to 0.5% from 1.0%; claims are expected in a 216,000-220,000 range after 225,000. The observable trigger is whether producer-price breadth moderates without a renewed drop in claims. Softer pipeline inflation with weaker labor demand would confirm that the Fed has room to wait and challenge the rise in 2- to 10-year real yields. Firm core PPI with low claims would confirm that June 10’s CPI relief was narrow, preserve the December-hike interpretation and reopen pressure on the belly.

The June 11 funding sequence will test whether the 10-year reopening’s strong end-user demand extends to both cash and long duration. Treasury will auction $75 billion each of 4- and 8-week bills at 11:30 ET, reopen $22 billion of 30-year bonds at 13:00, and may buy back up to $12.5 billion of near-term nominal coupons from 13:40 to 14:00. The observable triggers are the long bond’s stop against its when-issued yield, bidder shares and dealer takedown, followed separately by bill demand and the June 15 coupon settlement. Strong long-end bidding and orderly bills would confirm a macro-led steepening and resolve part of the buyer-capacity concern. A weak bond result despite benign inflation would challenge that account and reopen fiscal supply as an independent term-premium driver.

The ECB decision on June 11, Türkiye’s policy decision the same day and the BOJ meeting ending June 16 form the global policy test. the base case calls for a 25-basis-point ECB increase that takes the deposit rate to 2.25%, a Turkish repo hold at 37% despite effective funding near 40%, and a 25-basis-point BOJ increase toward 1.00%. Observable triggers are the guidance on follow-up moves, Bund and JGB curve reactions, lira and yen behavior, and the BOJ’s purchase plan. Contained guidance with stable currencies would challenge the view that foreign tightening must keep lifting the Treasury clearing rate. Hawkish sequencing, disorderly long-end selling or renewed reserve pressure would confirm global competition for duration and reopen imported-inflation risk across oil-importing sovereigns.

section_summaries.market_read: The close kept policy restraint concentrated in the front end and supply risk in duration.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether June 11 inflation, labor and auction results validate the stable front end.
section_summaries.summary: Soft core inflation gave the Fed room to wait but did not revive an easing path, leaving the front end steady while real yields and breakevens rose farther out. Strong 10-year auction demand contained the selloff, yet energy, deficits and global issuance preserved term-premium pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: A steady 2-year yield versus weaker long duration reinforced a wait-and-watch Fed path.
section_summaries.next_check: The 30-year auction will show whether end-user demand extends beyond the 10-year sector.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Policy-sensitive yields held near their post-payroll repricing as losses increased with maturity and the curve steepened modestly. Higher real yields and breakevens shared the nominal move, while orderly bills, a strong 10-year reopening and a larger fiscal deficit separated near-term absorption from persistent supply pressure.

section_summaries.market_read: Functional issuance limits systemic concern, but concentrated funding demand raises clearing costs.
section_summaries.next_check: Track whether new deals require wider concessions as portfolios rebuild duration capacity.
section_summaries.summary: Credit weakened modestly as government yields rose, yet primary markets still cleared large deals for borrowers with strong balance sheets or protected cash flows. Amazon’s financing and AI infrastructure issuance showed that access remains open while crowding, concessions and weaker-borrower refinancing risks become more uneven.

section_summaries.market_read: Global buyer capacity remained intact, but foreign alternatives kept the US long-end hurdle high.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch Bund and JGB curve reactions for evidence of stronger competition for global savings.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Weak demand at Japan’s super-long auction and record sovereign syndication underscored that global duration remains available only at a higher clearing price. Overseas policy tightening, fiscal expansion and competing high-grade supply kept pressure on Treasury term premium despite softer US core inflation and uneven global growth.

section_summaries.market_read: EM pressure remained country-specific, with energy importers most exposed to tighter financing.
section_summaries.next_check: Monitor lira, rupiah and reserve trends for signs that currency defense is losing traction.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: EM debt faced higher base yields and oil without a broad dollar squeeze, making domestic buffers and policy credibility decisive. Indonesia stabilized through currency-defense measures, while Türkiye and South Africa showed how reserve use, expensive funding and imported inflation can shift stress into sovereign financing and credit.

section_summaries.market_read: Risk-off assets no longer guaranteed duration support when inflation and real rates dominated.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether renewed equity weakness is met by lower real yields or continued long-end selling.
section_summaries.summary: Equities and technology sold off as volatility rose, yet Treasuries failed to rally because oil and real yields moved higher. A nearly flat dollar and falling gold pointed to a real-discount-rate shock rather than systemic fear, while modest credit losses showed capital markets remained functional.

section_summaries.market_read: The next signals will determine whether the steepening was macro-led or an independent supply shock.
section_summaries.next_check: Compare June 11 data, auction outcomes and global policy reactions through the June 16 BOJ close.
section_summaries.summary: Three June 11-16 tests will distinguish a temporary pause from renewed duration relief: US inflation and labor data, Treasury funding demand, and global central-bank guidance. Their market triggers span real yields, auction quality, foreign curves and currencies, directly testing whether macro restraint or supply competition dominates.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.21% (+1 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.34% (+1 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 42 bp (+2 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 90 bp (+2 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasury curve bear-steepens as soft core CPI fails to dislodge high real yields
overall_summary.session_narrative: At the completed NY close, the Treasury curve bear-steepened mildly as the front end held steady and longer yields rose. Soft core CPI gave the Fed room to wait, but persistent hike pricing, firmer oil, and higher real yields and breakevens blocked a duration rally. Strong 10-year auction demand stabilized the session; large fiscal needs, global issuance and financing-intensive investment kept the long-end clearing rate elevated.
overall_summary.summary: The front end held near its post-payroll repricing while longer Treasuries cheapened, preserving a high-real-rate regime. Softer core CPI bought time for the Fed, but oil, fiscal supply and global competition for duration prevented broader relief.

watch_events.market_implication: The first challenges higher real yields; the second preserves hike pricing and belly pressure.
watch_events.market_trigger: Producer-price breadth moderates while claims weaken, or core PPI stays firm as claims remain low.
watch_events.title: US PPI and jobless claims
watch_events.watch_line: Test whether softer pipeline inflation coincides with renewed labor-market weakening.

watch_events.market_implication: Strong demand contains term premium; weak demand revives fiscal supply as an independent driver.
watch_events.market_trigger: A through-market bond stop and orderly bills, versus a tail with elevated dealer takedown.
watch_events.title: Treasury bill, bond and buyback sequence
watch_events.watch_line: Assess long-bond bidding, bill demand, buyback execution and the June 15 cash settlement.

watch_events.market_implication: Stability lowers the global clearing-rate pressure; stress confirms competition for duration.
watch_events.market_trigger: Contained guidance and stable currencies, or hawkish moves with disorderly long-end selling.
watch_events.title: ECB, Türkiye and BOJ policy sequence
watch_events.watch_line: Track guidance, Bund and JGB curves, the BOJ purchase plan, and lira and yen behavior.
